mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable or declining federal funds rates indicates robust consumer demand supported by accommodative financing, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model, with a negative Sharpe ratio on 20-day forward returns across all tested interaction quadrants.

mechanism_narrative: An expanding industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into immediate positive equity momentum over the next 5 trading days during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, with a mean forward return below zero and a t-statistic less than 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding employment and wage growth, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases correlate with negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, raising discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation months fail to precede negative 60-day returns, with a positive mean return and insignificant predictive power with p > 0.05 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and industrial production signal synchronized strength across residential and manufacturing sectors, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in over 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2% annualized confirms sustained aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings revisions and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days during expansionary regimes.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2% fails to predict positive 60-day returns during expansions, with a negative mean return and a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.
